title: Second full corpus release + data-quality report
context: M2's capstone: a second full corpus release that incorporates the taxonomy classifier (cancer-type/study-type tags), the aggregate-vs-individual-data signal, and a data-quality report showing measurable improvement vs the M1 baseline. This release must demonstrate at least one verifiable external reuse event, confirming the corpus is being used by real beneficiaries.
objective: Produce and release a second full oncology corpus, incorporating M2 enrichments (cancer-type tags, study-type classification, data-availability signal), alongside a data-quality report measuring improvement vs the M1 baseline — with ≥ 1 verifiable external reuse event recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Second corpus release built deterministically from a pinned snapshot; manifest content hash recorded and reproducible on clean rebuild.
Cancer-type tags, study-type classifications, and data-availability signals populated on all applicable records; classifier scores on the gold set committed alongside the release.
Data-quality report documents measurable curation-quality improvement vs the M1 baseline release: at minimum, precision/recall improvement or coverage increase, with before/after metrics.
gate-002 and patient-008 applied across the full build; audit artifact shows 0 tier leakage and 0 patient-content leakage in redistributable bundles.
At least 1 verifiable external reuse event recorded: citation, Zenodo derivative, or public repo depending on the corpus — with externally verifiable evidence linked in the outcome ledger.
License+compliance reviewer and technical reviewer sign off before publication.
resources: PLAN.md (Roadmap M2; Success metrics — verifiable reuse event)
output: A second versioned corpus release: manifest JSON + license-segregated bundles + per-bundle LICENSE manifests + updated datasheet + data-quality report (vs M1 baseline) + committed gate audit artifact + acceptance/reuse evidence in the outcome ledger.
